label: Lateral epicondylitis without fracture or neurovascular red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated lateral epicondylitis or tennis elbow.
No fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, elbow instability, ulnar nerve symptoms, neurovascular compromise, infection, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Fracture or dislocation concern, tendon rupture, distal biceps or triceps concern, elbow instability, ulnar nerve symptoms, neurovascular compromise, septic joint or infection concern, open wound, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
must_not_miss: Distal biceps or triceps tendon rupture.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: AAOS supports lateral epicondylitis as an overuse elbow condition, typical activity-related symptoms, nonsurgical care, and separate handling when fracture, nerve, neck, or other soft-tissue causes are suspected. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated lateral epicondylitis with fracture, rupture, ulnar nerve, infection, and neurovascular red flags absent.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. AAOS supports lateral epicondylitis as an overuse elbow condition, typical activity-related symptoms, nonsurgical care, and separate handling when fracture, nerve, neck, or other soft-tissue causes are suspected. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated lateral epicondylitis with fracture, rupture, ulnar nerve, infection, and neurovascular red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated lateral epicondylitis or tennis elbow
no tendon rupture or ulnar nerve symptoms
